Oracc: HBTIN — Hellenistic Babylonia: Texts, Iconography, Names

BiMes 24, 54 [Legal][via hbtin]

Obverse
(date)
o 1'1'

[...] MU ⸢6-KAM₂⸣ mse-lu-⸢ku LUGAL⸣ [...]

(o 1') [...] 6th year Seleucus (being) king. [...]

o 2'2'

[md60-DINiṭ ša₂ MU-šu₂ ša₂-nu-u₂ mqe₂-ep]-lu-u₂-nu lu₂GAL lu₂SAG ša₂ UNUG⸢ki⸣

(o 2') Anu-uballiṭ, whose other name is Kephalon, the ša rēši of Uruk, [son of Anu-balassu-iqbi, descendant of Ah]ʾutu; Anu-belšunu, his brother, Te-e-a-X

o 3'3'

[A ša₂ md60-DIN-su-E A mŠEŠ]-ʾu-u₂-tu m⸢d60-EN-šu₂-nu ŠEŠ-šu₂⸣ mte-e-a-[...]

o 4'4'

[...] ⸢x⸣ E₂ ⸢ša₂ x⸣ [...]

(o 4') [...] the house of x [...] all of it [...] his brother ... [... in pe]rpetuity. ... his house plot

o 5'5'

[...]

o 6'6'

[...]

o 7'7'

[...] ⸢x x⸣

o 8'8'

[...] gab-bi

o 9'9'

[...] ⸢x x⸣ [x] ⸢x⸣ [...] ⸢ŠEŠ-šu₂⸣ [...]-da-ʾ

o 10'10'

[... a-na u₄]-⸢mu⸣ ṣa-a-tu₂ ⸢x⸣-[...]-⸢šu₂* x* x* be*-am*-ma*⸣ x x x ⸢qa*⸣-nu*-šu₂*

o 11'11'

[...] ⸢x x mki-din-d*⸣60*? ⸢gab⸣-[bi ...] na*-x*-šu₂* ul u₂-ba*-ʾa*-u₂

(o 11') [...] Kidin-Anu, all of it [...] bears (responsibility). They(?) will not search (for) [...] that was made.

Reverse
r 1r 1

[...]-šu₂ ša₂ ip-pu-uš lu-u₂-ʾu!(ḪI-)pu e-lat ša₂-ṭa-ri-šu₂-⸢nu⸣

(r 1) The x, in addition to their document [...] the gods of Uruk, which was performed, the sons of Labaši, son of

r 22

[...] ⸢DINGIR⸣-MEŠ ša₂ UNUGki ša₂ ip-pu-uš A-MEŠ ša₂ mNU-TEŠ₂ A ša₂

r 33

[...]-⸢x⸣ A! mŠU-d60 lib₃-bu-u₂ ša₂ ina IGI gu? su mNU-TEŠ₂ AD-šu₂-nu; i-pu-uš

(r 3) [...] descendant of Gimil-Anu ... Labaši, their father, he did.

about 2 lines uninscribed
r 44

[... m]⸢qe₂⸣-ep-lu-u₂-nu lu₂GAL lu₂SAG ša₂ UNUGki ina un-qa-šu₂

(r 4) [...] Kephalon, commander of Uruk, with his ring

r 55

[... mx]-mu-uq*-ra-te-e ina ⸢x-x⸣-uš ša₂ UNUGki ina un-qa-šu₂1

(r 5) [... Ti]mokrates, with ... of Uruk, with his ring

r 66

[...]-a lu₂UKKIN ša₂ UNUGki ina ARAD* E₂? ša₂ bi-rit-šu₂-nu

(r 6) [...] the assembly of Uruk, with the slave of the house that is between them

remainder uninscribed

1collation Monerie 2014: 127
